jokes.text: Fence? Honestly, nobody told me anything about a fence.
jokes.setup: Larry being told about a mandatory fence ordinance
jokes.punchline: His genuine bewilderment at a basic safety requirement
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry obliviousness to social norms and regulations, but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Trump has really ruined it for all Don Juniors, hasn't he?
jokes.setup: Meeting someone named Don Winston Jr.
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate observation about Trump ruining the name
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical observation that's perfectly in Larry's voice. Good topical humor without being preachy.

jokes.text: Who throws a funeral for themselves while they're alive?
jokes.setup: Albert Brooks having a 'live funeral'
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredulous reaction to the absurd concept
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the episode's central absurdity, classic Larry reaction to social weirdness.

jokes.text: to accelerate his demise
jokes.setup: Larry describing his Young Larry show concept about living with his uncle
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic description of trying to kill his uncle
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and economical. The euphemistic phrasing makes it funnier than direct language would. Peak Larry David.

jokes.text: Making him climb up six flights of stairs, telling him the elevator's broken.
jokes.setup: Examples of how he'd kill his uncle
jokes.punchline: Specific, petty method of senior abuse
jokes.explanation: Good specific detail that makes the dark premise more concrete and funny.

jokes.text: I drove this old lady around. She was blind. I never cleaned the car. She had no idea.
jokes.setup: Larry describing his chauffeur job
jokes.punchline: Taking advantage of his blind employer's disability
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Larry's opportunistic nature. Dark but not cruel, just pragmatic in a twisted way.

jokes.text: They're like horse thieves in the old west, aren't they?
jokes.setup: Discussing pandemic hoarders
jokes.punchline: Absurd historical comparison
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and unexpected historical analogy. Very Larry to make such grandiose comparisons.

jokes.text: Don't give me any notes. That's all. I'm not joking. I'm not joking. That's not a joke.
jokes.setup: Larry being pleasant about working with Netflix
jokes.punchline: Immediate shift to his true demanding nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry character moment. The repetition and insistence it's not a joke makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I better get the money now before he forgets.
jokes.setup: Learning Dennis has early onset dementia
jokes.punchline: Larry's opportunistic response to exploit the condition
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Larry's self-interested logic. Dark but delivered with such matter-of-fact reasoning it becomes absurdly funny.

jokes.text: What are you doing? Were you, like stalking me?
jokes.setup: Larry asking for his money back
jokes.punchline: Dennis accuses Larry of stalking for debt collection
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the conflict but fairly predictable response.

jokes.text: I got my Jews confused. She's funny. Anti-Semitic, but funny.
jokes.setup: Lucy Liu confusing Larry with Jerry Seinfeld
jokes.punchline: Larry's reaction calling it anti-Semitic but also complimenting her
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Larry's simultaneous offense and appreciation. The 'but funny' saves it from being preachy.

jokes.text: I haven't had sex in a year, so I'm really excited. Pull over. I'm done.
jokes.setup: Lucy Liu expressing excitement about the evening
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about her sex life, causing Larry to want to escape
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with Larry's extreme reaction. The abruptness of his response elevates it.

jokes.text: You can't stand that the praise is going to somebody else.
jokes.setup: Albert explaining why he wants a live funeral
jokes.punchline: Larry cutting through the pretense to Albert's real motivation
jokes.explanation: Sharp insight into Albert's narcissism. Larry as the truth-teller cutting through social niceties.

jokes.text: I said, I'm taking my baby, my baby to Asia.
jokes.setup: Leon explaining his GoFundMe success
jokes.punchline: The simplistic and manipulative pitch that somehow worked
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leon character moment. The repetition of 'my baby' makes it funnier and more manipulative.

jokes.text: You are so fucking skinny, your arms can't even hold a fucking glass.
jokes.setup: Wine spill incident
jokes.punchline: Leon's brutal assessment of Lucy Liu's physique as the cause
jokes.explanation: Classic Leon bluntness. The physical comedy setup makes his harsh assessment funnier.

jokes.text: I got a great idea. Why don't you two start a GoFundMe page?
jokes.setup: The couch cleaning cost discussion
jokes.punchline: Callback to Leon's earlier GoFundMe success
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure, though not particularly original. Works because of the immediate relevance.

jokes.text: Larry walking into the glass door
jokes.setup: Larry navigating what appears to be an open doorway
jokes.punchline: He walks directly into a glass door
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy that becomes crucial to the episode's relationship dynamics. High impact despite being predictable.

jokes.text: That's two feeble things in a fucking row. He's got one more left. I'ma drop his ass off at a nursing home.
jokes.setup: Larry's wine spill and glass door collision
jokes.punchline: Leon's three-strikes assessment of Larry's competence
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and perfectly in Leon's voice. The 'three strikes' structure with nursing home threat is brilliant.

jokes.text: You've had a big night. I just feel like it was a lot for you.
jokes.setup: Larry expecting romantic continuation after the date
jokes.punchline: Lucy treating him like an invalid after his mishaps
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy as Larry realizes his romantic prospects have evaporated due to his clumsiness.

jokes.text: Can I knit as well?
jokes.setup: Lucy's suggestions for his cozy evening at home
jokes.punchline: Larry sarcastically suggesting the most stereotypical old person activity
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that highlights how she's treating him

jokes.text: That's where you're going? This is the bathroom I use to drop the kids off at the pool.
jokes.setup: Larry discovering Leon in his bathroom
jokes.punchline: Leon's euphemism for defecation using pool terminology
jokes.explanation: Classic Leon crude humor with the pool euphemism being particularly apt given the actual pool context.

jokes.text: The resort pool is not available to me?
jokes.setup: Larry restricting Leon from one specific bathroom
jokes.punchline: Leon referring to it as a 'resort pool' like it's a luxury amenity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leon characterization, turning a bathroom restriction into hotel-like exclusivity. Great economy.

jokes.text: The resort pool is not available to me? It is not available to you ever. Never? Never. Never.
jokes.setup: Leon asking about using Larry's master bathroom
jokes.punchline: Larry's absolute refusal creating petty domestic tension
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics showing Larry's petty territorialism even with houseguests.

jokes.text: I'll be honest, AB, I haven't even seen any of your fuckin' movies.
jokes.setup: Albert asking if Leon's date has seen his movies
jokes.punchline: Leon's brutal honesty about not seeing Albert's work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leon moment - completely honest and socially inappropriate at the same time.

jokes.text: Then we can have a real funeral. A real one, okay? And people will say all these wonderful things about me. Yes, let's keep our fingers crossed.
jokes.setup: Albert suggesting Larry might die from a brain bleed
jokes.punchline: Larry's deadpan agreement to hope Albert dies
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with perfect deadpan delivery. The polite 'let's keep our fingers crossed' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: that little fat motherfucker, who rolled on the floor, like in circles going, that motherfucker never got no ass. I guarantee you.
jokes.setup: Discussing how the Three Stooges didn't get romantic success
jokes.punchline: Leon's crude but accurate assessment of Curly's romantic prospects
jokes.explanation: Leon's characteristically blunt analysis. The physical description of Curly is vivid and crude but accurate.

jokes.text: You tapped the door. You're never gonna tap her.
jokes.setup: Larry insisting he can still be sexually viable despite his clumsiness
jokes.punchline: Leon's pun connecting Larry's door collision to his romantic prospects
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay with perfect economy. The sexual innuendo combined with the door callback is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: You tapped the door. You're never gonna tap her.
jokes.setup: Larry insisting he can still 'tap' sexually
jokes.punchline: Leon's wordplay connecting his door collision to sexual failure
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with perfect economy. The double meaning of 'tap' creates a devastating punchline.

jokes.text: It's extortion.
jokes.setup: Reading the threatening note about casting someone's daughter
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact assessment of the situation
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of the obvious. Larry stating what everyone is thinking.

jokes.text: Someone's tryna come up. Someone's tryna get paid. It's extortion.
jokes.setup: Larry receives fence violation notice
jokes.punchline: Leon immediately recognizing it as a shakedown
jokes.explanation: Leon's street-smart assessment of the situation

jokes.text: I'm a living doll, as my mother would say.
jokes.setup: Marcos calling Larry a nice guy
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic self-assessment while being extorted
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor given the extortion context. The 'mother would say' adds a nice touch.

jokes.text: But I know Marsha Lifshitz is that girl right there eating those left over tacos.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining the role isn't right for Maria Sofia
jokes.punchline: Marcos's confident misidentification despite obvious mismatch
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment showing the father's delusion about casting appropriateness.

jokes.text: I remember what you wore. You wore a green sweater, tan pants, a blue and white checkered shirt. You had an almond decaf latte and a bran muffin. Now if I know all that, don't you think I would know if you paid me?
jokes.setup: Dennis claiming he already paid Larry back
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredibly detailed memory proving Dennis is lying
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Larry's obsessive memory and logical argument structure

jokes.text: I remember what you wore. You wore a green sweater, tan pants, a blue and white checkered shirt. You had an almond decaf latte and a bran muffin.
jokes.setup: Dennis claiming he paid Larry but Larry doesn't remember
jokes.punchline: Larry's obsessive recall of irrelevant details to prove his memory works
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy showing Larry's obsessive attention to detail. The specificity makes it funnier and more believable.

jokes.text: Yeah, I walked into the door. I didn't see the door. How could I? It looks like air. Everybody's done it.
jokes.setup: People suggesting Larry has memory problems like Dennis
jokes.punchline: Larry's defensive rationalization of walking into glass
jokes.explanation: Good callback and rationalization showing Larry's need to justify everything

jokes.text: Have I ever complimented you two guys on anything? No. I didn't tell you I like your new pink driver? No, you didn't. Must have been somebody else.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to prove Dennis is lying about compliments
jokes.punchline: The golf buddies confirming Larry never compliments anyone
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency joke. Larry's inability to give compliments is perfectly established.

jokes.text: We're fucked. It's a big bowl of fucked.
jokes.setup: Discussing the Maria Sofia casting situation
jokes.punchline: Larry's colorful metaphor for their predicament
jokes.explanation: Memorable phrase with good visual imagery. 'Big bowl of fucked' is an entertaining escalation.

jokes.text: You're a plopper! You've always been a plopper!
jokes.setup: Continuing argument about Susie spilling wine
jokes.punchline: Larry's accusatory escalation about Susie's sitting style
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurd argument. The repetition and accusatory tone make it funnier.

jokes.text: Jeff reenacting the plopping motion
jokes.setup: Larry asking Jeff to confirm the plopping
jokes.punchline: Jeff's exaggerated physical demonstration
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy callback but not particularly innovative or memorable.

jokes.text: I am not a bad guy. Not a bad guy. A good guy. A very good guy.
jokes.setup: Larry being kicked out of Jeff's car after the fight
jokes.punchline: Larry's desperate self-affirmation while being clearly unreasonable
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry self-delusion. The repetition and increasing desperation make it funny.

jokes.text: Bashert means fate. Like you wanna say I'm feeling a lot of tsuris.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to use Yiddish words incorrectly in his speech
jokes.punchline: Jon Hamm's correction and better suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Larry's language confusion, though fairly standard cultural humor.

jokes.text: The word that kept reappearing was repugnant.
jokes.setup: Explaining why the Larry David movie was canceled
jokes.punchline: The brutal audience feedback about Larry's character
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor about Larry's public persona. 'Repugnant' is perfectly chosen for maximum impact.

jokes.text: I don't see you in a sexual way anymore.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to understand why Lucy is treating him differently
jokes.punchline: Lucy's blunt admission about his diminished sexual appeal
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the glass door setup. Lucy's directness makes it more brutal and funny.

jokes.text: And I could prove it to you. Let's go upstairs. Come on. I'll prove it to ya. You wanna test it? Test me. Come on. I dare ya.
jokes.setup: Larry defending his virility to Lucy Liu
jokes.punchline: His desperate public challenge to test his sexual competence
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with Larry's public desperation. The escalation to a sexual dare is appropriately uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Watch out for glass.
jokes.setup: Lucy leaving Larry at the funeral
jokes.punchline: Her sarcastic parting shot referencing his earlier mishap
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback timing and delivery. Two words that encapsulate their entire relationship dynamic.

jokes.text: It would've given Albert tremendous satisfaction to see all these people gathered here to pay their last respects to a man they will see again in less than an hour.
jokes.setup: Traditional funeral eulogy opening
jokes.punchline: Highlighting the absurdity of a living person's funeral
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the logical absurdity of the situation. Well-structured irony.

jokes.text: I'm very sorry that Albert is faux-dead. 'Cause I was going to leave him some faux money.
jokes.setup: Larry giving his speech at Albert's fake funeral
jokes.punchline: The 'faux-dead/faux money' parallel
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Larry's sarcastic approach to the ridiculous situation.

jokes.text: Albert is the one who inspired me to become a stand-up comedian. And that, more than anything, makes me wish he was really dead.
jokes.setup: Larry acknowledging Albert's influence on his career
jokes.punchline: Wishing Albert was dead because of that inspiration
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and perfectly structured. The setup creates genuine sentiment before the brutal punchline. Peak Larry David.

jokes.text: He's a fuckin' COVID hoarder. Albert's a COVID hoarder.
jokes.setup: Discovery of Albert's closet full of supplies
jokes.punchline: Callback to earlier COVID hoarder discussion
jokes.explanation: Strong callback that pays off the earlier setup perfectly. Leon's outrage makes it funnier.

jokes.text: motherfucking office buildings and shit, they ostracized from the other pigeons and shit... 'Cause that goofy motherfucker ran into a goddamn building last week
jokes.setup: Leon explaining animal behavior parallels to Larry's situation
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphor about pigeon social dynamics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leon monologue with creative animal behavior metaphor. The specificity and crude language make it hilarious.

jokes.text: You're forgettin' one key thing here. Free. Who wouldn't wanna go to Asia for free?
jokes.setup: Larry questioning Leon's Mary Ferguson replacement plan
jokes.punchline: Leon's simple but logical argument about free travel
jokes.explanation: Good Leon logic - crude but undeniably practical. The simplicity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Let me tell you something, dementia boy, you gave me a lifetime guarantee and I don't care if you don't remember your dog's fucking name.
jokes.setup: Dennis claiming he doesn't remember giving a lifetime guarantee
jokes.punchline: Susie's brutal exploitation of his memory issues
jokes.explanation: 'Dementia boy' is brutal and 'dog's fucking name' is a perfect escalation. Classic Susie ruthlessness.

jokes.text: Here, keep it. I got another one.
jokes.setup: Lucy asking for Purell
jokes.punchline: Larry casually giving away hoarded sanitizer, contradicting his earlier judgment
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Larry's hypocrisy. The casual delivery makes his contradiction funnier.
